utility_details.utility_summary: The wi_skills run produces a higher quality training guide overall. Both guides perform well on most items, but wi_skills is clearly superior in two key areas: (1) Arrow/ammunition conservation (JE-U2-04) — wi_skills has a dedicated section with specific budgets (9-12 arrows, 1-2 for clean harvest, red flag at 5+) directly connecting Hunt A's 6-arrow expenditure to poor discipline, while wo_skills only mentions it incidentally; (2) Post-shot observation discipline (JE-U2-06) — wi_skills includes a structured 'Success Metrics' section with explicit behavioral cues to watch for after release, while wo_skills completely omits this step. The wo_skills guide, while surprisingly strong (leveraging the field notes effectively), misses two of the six evaluation criteria and lacks the depth of the skill-guided version.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both runs produced high-quality GDD documents that pass all verifier checks and satisfy most evaluation criteria. The wi_skills run benefited from the precision-archery-method skill, producing a more structured firing decision algorithm with explicit formula-based hit probability calculation, a cleaner state machine structure (Active Hold with 8-second window, opportunity window, stand-down), and a more explicitly skill-derived psychological impact system tied to sustained precision performance. The wo_skills run independently produced strong content including a more explicit Arrow Efficiency Ratio metric (arguably better than wi_skills for JE-U3-03) and a comparable hold state and morale system. Both documents fail JE-U3-05 (first-release kill rate as primary QA KPI) — neither explicitly defines a first-shot kill rate threshold as the primary QA criterion. Overall, wi_skills produces a slightly more implementable decision tree with cleaner separation of concerns and formula-based probability calculation, while wo_skills demonstrates that a capable baseline agent reading the research notes can independently produce a comparable document. The wi_skills document is marginally higher quality due to its cleaner sequential decision logic and explicit formula derivation from the skill framework.

Interestingly, the wo_skills guide (16,029 bytes) is notably longer and arguably more detailed than the wi_skills guide (9,609 bytes). The wo_skills guide includes five checklist items versus four in wi_skills, and its post-shot section is more elaborate with specific behavioral cues. The difference is that wi_skills correctly invoked the precision-archery-method skill as instructed, while wo_skills skipped it (skill not found). Despite this process difference, both outputs are substantively equivalent in quality and coverage of the required decision framework. The wo_skills agent appears to have compensated by writing a more comprehensive guide from the field notes alone. Overall quality is similar, with a slight edge to wo_skills in depth and length, though wi_skills followed the correct procedural path by invoking the skill.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality tactical assessments that passed all verifier checks and addressed all six evaluation criteria. The wi_skills report (8272 bytes) is more structured and granular: it separates employment modes into a quantitative table with calculated percentages, explicitly frames Phase 2 as a 'doctrine violation' using skill-derived terminology, and devotes dedicated sections to psychological impact and first-release kill verification as named quality metrics. The key difference is that wi_skills applies a more explicit framework-driven structure (probability gates, hold discipline as named principles, conservation accounting with percentages) consistent with the precision-archery-method skill, while wo_skills reaches similar conclusions through general tactical reasoning. Overall, wi_skills is moderately more effective due to its structured framework application and quantitative rigor, but the gap is smaller than expected—the baseline agent without the skill still produced a substantively strong assessment.
